cards.back: A cryptographic method that uses a random number to generate a unique key, which is used only once to encrypt and decrypt a message

cards.front: When was the One-time Pad invented
cards.back: The One-time Pad was invented by Gilbert Vernam in 1917

cards.front: What makes the One-time Pad secure
cards.back: The One-time Pad is secure because it uses a random key that is unknown to anyone except the sender and the intended recipient, making it virtually unbreakable

cards.front: What is the key length requirement for a One-time Pad
cards.back: The key length of a One-time Pad must be at least as long as the message being encrypted to ensure secure encryption

cards.front: Is the One-time Pad still used today
cards.back: Yes, the One-time Pad is still used today in certain high-security applications, such as military communications and financial transactions, due to its unbreakable encryption
